fall_red_flags: Loss of consciousness after a fall is an emergency red flag.
Patients on blood thinners with headache or head impact should be treated as high risk.
Difficulty breathing, severe bleeding, or inability to move after a fall requires urgent escalation.
first_aid_fall: Do not move the person if spinal injury is suspected unless there is immediate danger.
Control visible bleeding with firm pressure if safe to do so.
Call emergency services if the person is unconscious, confused, bleeding heavily, or in severe pain.
cpr: If the person is not breathing normally and unresponsive, begin hands-only CPR.
Push hard and fast in the center of the chest at about 100 to 120 compressions per minute.
Continue until emergency services arrive or the person resumes normal breathing.
